description: Close the provider/model-routing config gap: add `task-mgr models set-review-model`/`unset-review-model` and `set-fallback`/`unset-fallback` subcommands (today reviewModel and fallbackRunner are hand-edited JSON only), and extend `task-mgr models show` to print the full routing table (default+source, reviewModel, fallbackRunner, primaryRunner byTaskType/byIdPrefix routes). Makes 'where does each task class route?' answerable and safely settable from the CLI. Aligns with the config-first principle (config owns class/role routing; per-task model stamps are exceptions).

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`
Rust: Scoped tests pass with `cargo test -p task-mgr <module>`
No literal model-string creep outside src/loop_engine/model.rs (tests/no_hardcoded_models.rs guards this) — read model IDs from the model.rs constants
Config writes preserve ALL pre-existing keys (round-trip via serde_json::Value, never a typed reserialize)
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — ~1 day now to save ~2+ weeks later (1:10+ ratio); we are pre-launch, foundations compound
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable, wired in per plan
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — qualityDimensions on every task tells you what 'good' looks like
Config-first routing — reviewModel/fallbackRunner/primaryRunner own class routing; CLI setters write those, they don't stamp tasks
Edge cases = test cases — every known edge case must have a corresponding test
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at REVIEW-001 — REVIEW must leave the trunk green including pre-existing failures
Ship working code with tests to prove it; handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Tests that mirror implementation internals (break when refactoring)

A config setter that deserializes to a typed struct and reserializes — it silently DROPS unknown keys (additionalAllowedTools, embeddingModel, ollamaUrl, …). Setters MUST round-trip via serde_json::Value and mutate only the target key.
Error messages that don't identify the bad value or the allowed set
A startup probe / validation that diverges from the runtime resolver (binary resolution must mirror exactly)

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: write_project_review_model(Some("claude-opus-4-7")) sets reviewModel and leaves every other key byte-intact
Positive: write_*_review_model(None) and write_*_fallback_runner(None) remove the key and succeed even if it was absent
Positive: writing to an absent config file creates it with the target key + version
Negative: unrelated keys (additionalAllowedTools, embeddingModel, ollamaUrl, rerankerModel) are PRESERVED across a set (the load-bearing test)
Negative: malformed existing JSON returns an Err with the file path, not a silent overwrite
Known-bad: a typed-struct reserialize would pass a 'reviewModel == opus' assertion but drop additionalAllowedTools — the test must assert a pre-seeded additionalAllowedTools array survives
CONTRACT: project/user config paths resolved via the same helper the config loader uses (grep the loader to confirm parity)
userStories.description:
Add key-preserving config setters that write/clear `reviewModel` and `fallbackRunner`, mirroring the existing default-model writers.

DO:
- Reuse the pattern of `write_project_default` / `write_user_default` (src/commands/init/mod.rs). Add `write_project_review_model(Option<&str>)`, `write_user_review_model(Option<&str>)`, and `write_project_fallback_runner(Option<&FallbackRunnerConfig>)` / user equivalent. `None` clears the key.
- Read the existing config file as `serde_json::Value`, mutate ONLY the target key (insert or remove), and write back with stable formatting. This preserves additionalAllowedTools/embeddingModel/ollamaUrl/etc.
- Resolve the project config path the same way the loader does (project `.task-mgr/config.json`) and the user config path the same way (`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/task-mgr/config.json`). Create parent dirs if missing.

DO NOT:
- Deserialize into ProjectConfig and reserialize — that drops unknown keys (PROHIBITED).
- Duplicate path-resolution logic — reuse the loader's path helpers.

Edge cases:
- Config file absent: create it with just the target key (+ version).
- Config file present with unrelated keys: those survive untouched.
- Clearing a key that isn't present: no-op, success.
- Malformed existing JSON: return a clear error, do not silently overwrite.

Known-bad to guard against:
- A round-trip through the typed ProjectConfig struct that compiles and passes a naive 'reviewModel set' assertion but silently deletes additionalAllowedTools.
edgeCases: Config absent: create with target key + version
Malformed JSON: clear error, no overwrite

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Err with file path; original file untouched

userStories.notes: Reuse: write_project_default/write_user_default (src/commands/init/mod.rs) — check whether they already round-trip via Value (mirror it); ProjectConfig/FallbackRunnerConfig (src/loop_engine/project_config.rs). Config path resolution: src/db/path.rs + the project_config loader. Learning 3108-adjacent: keep writer/reader path resolution in lockstep.
qualityDimensions: Round-trip via serde_json::Value — never drop unknown keys
Path resolution shared with the loader (no divergence)
None clears; absent file creates; malformed errors clearly
Functions <30 lines, DI-friendly (path injectable for tests)
userStories.title: Config write/unset helpers for reviewModel + fallbackRunner (project + user level)

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `models set-review-model claude-opus-4-7` writes reviewModel (user scope) and prints a confirmation; `--project` writes project scope
Positive: `models set-fallback --enable --provider grok --model grok-build` writes the fallbackRunner block; `unset-fallback` removes it
Positive: setting a grok review/fallback model probes the grok binary and errors (NO write) if it's missing — mirrors check_review_model_binary / resolve_and_verify_grok_binary exactly
Negative: `set-fallback --provider openai` is rejected in v1 with a clear 'only grok' message and no write
Negative: probe-before-write — a missing-binary case leaves the config file unchanged (assert original bytes)
Unit test: set-review-model then unset-review-model round-trips to the original config (other keys intact)
Edge: setting reviewModel while a primaryRunner provider:"codex" route exists prints a warning that the engine will reject the combo at load
CONTRACT: the grok-binary probe here calls the SAME resolver as the loop startup probe (grep both to confirm)
userStories.description:
Add CLI subcommands that write the routing config via FEAT-001 helpers, with validation that mirrors runtime.

DO:
- Extend the models subcommand enum (src/cli/commands.rs) and handlers (src/commands/models/handlers.rs), mirroring handle_set_default. `--project` writes project config, default writes user config (match set-default's convention).
- `set-review-model <id>` / `unset-review-model`: validate the id is a known/plausible model; if `provider_for_model(id) == Grok`, probe the Grok binary (reuse resolve_and_verify_grok_binary / check_review_model_binary, project_config.rs) and fail with a helpful error if absent.
- `set-fallback`: flags `--enable/--disable`, `--provider <grok>`, `--model <id>`, `--cli-binary <path>`, `--runtime-error-threshold <n>`. In v1 reject `--provider` other than `grok` (matches the engine's v1 contract). When enabling, probe the binary. `unset-fallback` removes the block.
- Emit a confirmation line via ui::emit_data naming what was set and the scope (project/user), matching set-default's output style.

DO NOT:
- Bypass FEAT-001 helpers (no inline JSON writing here).
- Probe a binary the runtime wouldn't (mirror the resolver exactly).
- Accept a non-grok fallback provider in v1.

Edge cases:
- set-review-model to a grok model with grok binary missing: error before writing (don't persist an unusable config).
- set-fallback --enable without --model: default to grok-build (the engine default) or require it — require it and error clearly if absent.
- unset on an already-absent key: success no-op.
- A primaryRunner Codex route present + setting reviewModel: surface a warning (the engine rejects this combo at load) — non-fatal here, but warn.

Known-bad to guard against:
- Writing the config FIRST then probing — a missing binary would leave a persisted config that fails at next loop startup. Probe BEFORE write.
edgeCases: grok model + missing binary: error before write
set-fallback --enable without --model: require it, clear error
reviewModel set while codex primary route present: warn (engine rejects at load)

failureModes.cause: Write before probe with missing binary
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Forbidden — probe first; config left unchanged on probe failure
Rejected with explicit message, no write

userStories.notes: Reuse: handle_set_default (src/commands/models/handlers.rs:~140), the models subcommand enum (src/cli/commands.rs), provider_for_model + resolve_and_verify_grok_binary + check_review_model_binary (src/loop_engine/project_config.rs:376/430), FEAT-001 setters. Model IDs from model.rs constants (no literals). Note the v1 'fallback provider must be grok' rule matches the in-flight codex-runner PRD's config validation — keep the message consistent.
qualityDimensions: Probe BEFORE write (never persist an unusable config)
Validation mirrors runtime resolver exactly (shared helpers)
v1 fallback provider restricted to grok with a clear message
Output style matches existing set-default
No inline JSON writing — delegates to FEAT-001
userStories.title: `models set-review-model`/`unset-review-model` + `set-fallback`/`unset-fallback` subcommands

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `models show` prints the resolved default+source AND a routing block with reviewModel, fallbackRunner, and primaryRunner routes
Positive: reviewModel row shows the resolved provider (provider_for_model) e.g. 'claude-opus-4-7 (provider: Claude)'
Positive: every primaryRunner byTaskType/byIdPrefix entry is listed as 'X -> provider/model'
Negative: unset reviewModel / disabled fallback / empty primaryRunner each print an explicit empty-state row (not omitted)
Negative: output is ui::emit_data (stdout CLI data), not tracing
Unit test: a config with reviewModel=opus + fallbackRunner(grok-build) + byIdPrefix{FIX:grok-build} renders all three sections with correct provider labels
Unit test: an empty config renders explicit (unset)/disabled/(no routes) rows
CONTRACT: routing display reads the same ProjectConfig fields the loop loads (grep ProjectConfig to confirm field names)
userStories.description:
Extend `task-mgr models show` to answer 'where does each task class route?' in one command.

DO:
- Keep the existing 'resolved default + source' output, then append a Routing section: reviewModel (and resolved provider via provider_for_model), fallbackRunner (enabled/provider/model/threshold or 'disabled'), and every primaryRunner route (byTaskType entries and byIdPrefix entries, each showing prefix/type -> provider+model).
- Render via ui::emit_data (CLI data on stdout) consistent with the existing show output; stable, greppable formatting.
- Show 'unset'/'disabled'/'(none)' explicitly so absence is visible, not silently empty.

DO NOT:
- Recompute routing with bespoke logic — read the same ProjectConfig the loader produces and label provider via provider_for_model / parse_config_provider so the display matches actual runtime routing.
- Emit via tracing (this is CLI data, use ui::emit_data per CONTRACT-LOG-001).

Edge cases:
- No routing config at all: print 'reviewModel: (unset)', 'fallbackRunner: disabled', 'primaryRunner: (no routes)'.
- primaryRunner with overlapping byIdPrefix keys: show them as configured (don't try to resolve the overlap; note matching is byTaskType-first then byIdPrefix).
- reviewModel set to a grok model: show 'reviewModel: grok-build (provider: Grok)'.

Known-bad to guard against:
- A view that prints only set keys and omits unset ones — the operator can't tell 'unset' from 'forgot to print'. Always print every row with an explicit empty state.
edgeCases: Overlapping byIdPrefix keys: shown as configured, note byTaskType-first

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Forbidden — every row prints with an explicit empty state

userStories.notes: Reuse: handle_show (src/commands/models/handlers.rs), ProjectConfig (src/loop_engine/project_config.rs), provider_for_model (src/loop_engine/model.rs:83), ui::emit_data (CONTRACT-LOG-001). Mirror the existing show formatting. If the in-flight codex-runner PRD lands a parse_config_provider, label providers with it for codex-awareness; otherwise provider_for_model is fine.
qualityDimensions: Reads the same ProjectConfig the loop loads (no bespoke routing recompute)
Explicit empty states (unset/disabled/none) — absence is visible
ui::emit_data stdout, greppable/stable formatting
Provider labels match runtime classification
userStories.title: `models show` routing-table view

acceptanceCriteria: No code duplication — the Value-round-trip write core is shared across reviewModel/fallbackRunner/default writers
Functions under 30 lines
Validation reuses provider_for_model + the grok-binary resolver (no reimplementation)
Any issues found spawn REFACTOR-FIX-xxx via `task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001`
userStories.description:
Review the config setters + show extension for DRY, complexity, coupling.

DO: Check the three config-write helpers share one Value-round-trip core (not three copies); check CLI handler validation reuses the same resolver helpers; check show formatting isn't duplicating ProjectConfig field access.
DO NOT: Skip. Do NOT introduce a config-write abstraction with only one real use.
userStories.notes: If issues found: `echo '{...}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001` (priority 50-97). If clean, invoke /simplify on any ugly touchpoint, then emit `<task-status>REFACTOR-001:done</task-status>`. Do NOT stamp model on spawned fixups — primaryRunner.byIdPrefix config routes them.
qualityDimensions: Shared Value-round-trip core (DRY)
Validation reuses runtime resolvers
Single-responsibility functions
userStories.title: Review for refactoring opportunities

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: new subcommands dispatch from the clap enum to their handlers (grep-verified, not just defined)
Positive: FULL cargo test, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check all clean
Positive: every config write preserves unrelated keys (re-verify the FEAT-001 property end-to-end)
Positive: CLAUDE.md `task-mgr models` section + generated command-reference updated for set-review-model/unset-review-model/set-fallback/unset-fallback and the show routing view
Negative: no unwrap() in production paths; config writes never overwrite a malformed file silently
Negative: no literal model strings outside model.rs (no_hardcoded_models.rs green)
Pre-existing test failures fixed (or spawned as FIX-xxx with verifyCommand if >~12 unrelated)
If issues found: FIX-xxx/WIRE-FIX-xxx spawned via `task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001`
userStories.description:
Review all changes for quality, security, integration wiring, and docs. RUN THE FULL QUALITY GATE (unscoped suite).

DO: Verify the new subcommands are registered and reachable (clap dispatch → handler); verify key-preservation on every config write (the load-bearing property); verify probe-before-write; verify show reads the real ProjectConfig. Run full cargo test / clippy -D warnings / fmt --check (no scope filter). Update CLAUDE.md (the `task-mgr models` section) + the command-reference table for the new subcommands.
DO NOT: scope the suite — this is where the full suite runs. Do NOT stamp a model on this task (runtime reviewModel governs the reviewer).
userStories.notes: Do NOT stamp `model` here — runtime `reviewModel` config (currently claude-opus-4-7) owns the reviewer. Spawn fixes via `echo '{...}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001` (no model stamp on them — primaryRunner config routes them). If clean: `<task-status>REVIEW-001:done</task-status>` 'Clean review'. If gen-docs covers the command-reference table, run `cargo run --bin gen-docs` and verify `gen-docs -- --check` is green.
qualityDimensions: New subcommands wired clap→handler (reachable)
Key-preservation holds end-to-end
No unwrap in production; no silent overwrite of malformed config
Docs + command reference updated
Full suite green including pre-existing
userStories.title: Code review + final verification

acceptanceCriteria: Exactly one Value-round-trip atomic key-writer implementation exists in the crate (grep for the tempfile + persist + insert/remove pattern returns a single function body)
All six setters (write_default_model + write_review_model + write_fallback_runner across project_config + user_config) delegate to the shared helper
Each setter function is under 30 lines

Existing key-preservation tests pass unmodified: set_review_model_project_scope_writes_key_and_preserves_others, unset_review_model_project_scope_removes_key_preserves_others, set_fallback_writes_all_provided_fields_and_preserves_unrelated_keys, unset_fallback_removes_block_preserves_other_keys
No behavior change: project setters still seed {"version":1} on absent file, user setters still seed {}, atomic tempfile-persist still produces a trailing newline
userStories.description: Two near-identical write_config_key_at impls exist (src/loop_engine/project_config.rs:692-740 and src/loop_engine/user_config.rs:122-168), differing only in the empty-file seed default ({"version":1} vs {}). Additionally write_default_model in BOTH files (project_config.rs:641-684, user_config.rs:75-115) reimplements the same Value round-trip logic instead of calling the existing per-file helper. Net: four implementations of the same atomic Value-round-trip writer pattern. The acceptance criterion for REFACTOR-001 is that this core be shared across reviewModel/fallbackRunner/default writers. Extracting one helper with six real call sites (default + review + fallback across project + user) is well within the 'no one-use abstraction' guardrail.
userStories.title: Extract one shared Value-round-trip atomic key writer for all six config setters

acceptanceCriteria: handle_show_to is under 30 lines
Per-section render helpers are each under 30 lines

Every existing show_tests assertion passes unchanged (exact strings: 'reviewModel:    {model} (provider: ...)', 'fallbackRunner: enabled, provider=...', 'byIdPrefix[FIX] -> Grok/grok-build', '(unset)', 'disabled', '(no routes)')
Provider-label rendering pattern (parse_config_provider + map + unwrap_or) appears exactly once in the primary-runner section, shared across byTaskType and byIdPrefix
userStories.description: handle_show_to in src/commands/models/handlers.rs:185-272 is 88 lines, well over the 30-line ceiling REFACTOR-001 set as an acceptance criterion. It already breaks into four logical render sections (default model + db_dir header, reviewModel, fallbackRunner, primaryRunner). Extracting one helper per section keeps the function flat and readable while satisfying the AC. The provider-label pattern (parse_config_provider(...).map(provider_label).unwrap_or(spec.provider.as_str()) + writeln) repeats inside the primaryRunner branch for byTaskType and byIdPrefix — fold it through a tiny inner helper or a single loop over the two maps to remove the repetition.
userStories.title: Split handle_show_to into per-section render helpers to drop under 30 lines
